text.title: php-laravel-queues Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for php-laravel-queues as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Missing ShouldQueue interface on a job class: class ProcessOrderJob { use Dispatchable, Queueable... } with no implements ShouldQueue.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: dispatch() runs the job synchronously on the current thread. HTTP tests pass because execution is identical. In production, the queue never receives the work, and the HTTP thread blocks for the job's duration.

failure-mode.description: Passing Eloquent model instances or DTOs containing closures into job constructors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SerializesModels serializes via GlobalID and re-fetches on the worker. If the row was deleted between enqueue and run, findOrFail throws before handle() is called. Closures are never serializable — PHP throws immediately on dispatch().

failure-mode.description: Dispatching a job inside a DB::transaction without afterCommit().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The job is pushed to the queue before the transaction commits. The worker picks it up and calls findOrFail($id) — the row does not exist yet — and the job fails with a ModelNotFoundException.

failure-mode.description: No failed() implementation on a job that mutates external state (payment, webhook, inventory).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When the job exceeds $tries, it enters the dead queue with no application-level record. The user sees no error, the operator sees only a queue depth spike, and the failed state is never surfaced in the UI.

failure-mode.description: Using Queue::fake() for the only test of a job, never testing failed() behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Queue::fake() asserts that dispatch was called but never runs handle() or failed(). A job whose failed() silently does nothing passes all tests but leaves inconsistent state in production after a queue failure.

failure-mode.description: Missing failed_jobs migration on a project using queues.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without the failed_jobs table, when a job exceeds its retry limit Laravel throws a DB error on the storage attempt and the job is silently discarded — no record, no alert, no retry possible.
